run_summary.summary: Apr 21 catch-up — the Apr 18-21 Brandi runs committed to a detached git HEAD and never reached main or the sync system. Syncing now. Two urgents: (1) SOJOS Reel 1 draft is due TOMORROW Apr 23 — Ruby resent requirements Apr 20 (video ≥10 sec, ≥half close-ups of sunglasses, outdoor/car background, @sojosvision tag, #sojos #amazonsunglasses, MISSLIZSOJOS 10% code). (2) Flexsteel/Popfly application is BLOCKED — Ian asked Kenny for a screenshot of the managerial access prompt (Apr 20 PM) so he can investigate with his dev team. Also: Linkind concept awaiting brand approval (original Apr 15 deadline passed), Bedsure still no Iris response after two follow-ups, Ollie's Group 2 hard deadline Apr 29.

suggestions.message: Reel 1 draft is due TOMORROW (Apr 23)! Ruby resent requirements Apr 20: video ≥10 sec total, ≥half screen time must be close-ups of sunglasses, outdoor or car background, tag @sojosvision, hashtags #sojos + #amazonsunglasses, MISSLIZSOJOS 10% off code in caption. Time to film!

Popfly application BLOCKED — platform requires full IG business manager access. Kenny pushed back Apr 20. Ian replied Apr 20 8:42 PM: he says neither the brand nor Popfly would have that access and thinks it may be a platform bug. He needs a screenshot of exactly where the 'managerial access' prompt is appearing so his dev team can investigate. Send screenshot to ian@popfly.com today.

Group 2 content hard deadline is April 29 — 8 days away. Bring the Permission to Film Slip to the store. Submit draft via campaign manager before posting. Reminder: don't make it sound like a sale ('Ollie's doesn't have sales, our prices are low every day').

Liz sent a detailed concept breakdown Apr 20 (Top 3 Tips, Linkind as #1, lights 50%+ screen time). No concept approval response yet — original Apr 15 deadline passed with no written extension. Follow up with the platform if no response by Apr 22.
Liz sent $4,500 rate quote to Iris (MakeWonder MCN) Apr 13 and followed up Apr 20 — still no response. If Iris doesn't reply by Apr 23, worth one more nudge or moving on.

deals.next_action: Film Reel 1 and submit draft to Ruby for approval (due Apr 23)
deals.notes:
[Apr 20]: Ruby replied after Liz signed contract — resent Reel 1 requirements: video ≥10 sec, ≥half screen time must be close-ups of sunglasses, outdoor or car background, tag @sojosvision, hashtags #sojos + #amazonsunglasses, MISSLIZSOJOS 10% off code in caption.
[Apr 17]: Liz signed contract and returned to Ruby. Deal: 2 IG Reels for $2,250 total. Reel 1 due ~one week after receiving sunglasses (Kenny clarified language — flexible, not a fixed calendar date). Reel 2 due June 4 if invited to Amazon event, otherwise during Prime Day. Sunglasses (4 pairs) already received.
deals.activity_note: Apr 20: Ruby resent Reel 1 requirements after contract signed — draft due Apr 23. Specs: ≥10 sec, ≥half close-ups, outdoor/car background, @sojosvision tag, #sojos + #amazonsunglasses, MISSLIZSOJOS code in caption.

deals.next_action: Send Ian screenshot of the Popfly 'managerial access' prompt so he can investigate with dev team
deals.notes:
[Apr 20 8:42 PM]: Ian replied to Kenny's access pushback — says neither the brand nor Popfly would have account access; suspects it may be a platform bug. Asked Kenny to send a screenshot of where the managerial access prompt is appearing so he can check with his dev team.
[Apr 20 6:09 PM]: Kenny emailed Ian — Popfly campaign application requires full 'managerial access' to Liz's Instagram business account via FB Business Manager. Kenny pushed back: scope too broad, concerned about accidental algorithm changes; offered insights/whitelisting as alternative.
[Apr 17-20]: Popfly account created, application submitted. Also applied to HydroFlask campaign on marketplace. Filming for 3rd video: May 13-14 at Steinhafels Downers Grove, IL (Kenny's dad has pacemaker follow-up May 11, filming day after he returns May 12). End-of-May deadline for 3rd post per agreement.
deals.activity_note: Apr 20: Popfly application blocked — platform requires full IG manager access. Ian responded to Kenny's pushback — thinks it may be a bug, needs screenshot to investigate. On us to send screenshot to ian@popfly.com.

deals.next_action: Film and submit Group 2 content draft via campaign manager (hard deadline Apr 29)
deals.notes: [Apr 15]: Group 2 brief received — hard content deadline April 29. Bring Permission to Film Slip to the store for in-store filming. Submit draft via campaign manager before posting. Do NOT make it sound like a sale — 'Ollie's doesn't have sales, our prices are low every day.' Contact: Aviva. E-gift card en route.
deals.activity_note: Apr 15: Ollie's Group 2 brief — hard deadline Apr 29 for content submission. Permission to Film Slip required for store visit.

deals.next_action: Submit remaining Aleve video drafts (3 total — brand confirmed flexible on timing)
deals.notes: [Apr 13]: Concept approved and uploaded to content drive. Targeting 2 videos by end of week of Apr 14-18, 3rd by Apr 20. Brand confirmed flexible on timing for 3rd video.
deals.activity_note: Apr 13: Aleve concept approved — 3 videos in progress. 2 targeting week of Apr 14-18, 3rd flexible per brand.

deals.next_action: Follow up on concept approval if no response by Apr 22 — ready to film once approved
deals.notes:
[Apr 20 PM]: Platform relayed brand feedback — client wants lights to be majority of screen time. Liz replied clarifying her Top 3 Tips concept: Linkind as #1 tip, lights featured 50%+ of video (assembly, app screen recordings, day/night footage, key features). Awaiting final concept approval.
[Apr 20 AM]: Liz followed up with platform asking if concept was approved; said she can film that day.
[Apr 14]: Platform requested detailed concept by next morning. Liz sent generalized concept but pushed back on detail level relative to $2K payment.
[Apr 6]: Contract revisions — Liz requested posting deadline extended (was Apr 15) and revisions capped at 2 rounds. Platform said 'no problem' but no written confirmation of new deadline.
[Apr 1]: Contract sent for review.
[Mar 31]: Deal offered and accepted via socialmediacollabs.com — $2,000 for 1x 60–90 sec dedicated IG Reel reviewing Linkind SP6 Smart Solar Pathway Lights.
deals.activity_note: New deal — $2,000 Linkind SP6 Smart Solar Pathway Lights IG Reel via socialmediacollabs.com. Agreed Mar 31, contract revised Apr 6, concept submitted Apr 14 and clarified Apr 20. Awaiting brand concept approval. Original Apr 15 deadline passed; no written extension confirmed.

deals.next_action: Follow up with Iris if no reply by Apr 23
deals.notes:
[Apr 20]: Liz followed up to confirm Iris received rate quote — still no response since Apr 13.
[Apr 13]: Liz replied with rate: $4,500 bundle (1 IG Reel + 14-day link in bio + 90-day paid usage rights). Itemized: $2,500/Reel + $1,000/30-day paid usage.
[Apr 10–13]: Iris from MakeWonder MCN (official Bedsure partner agency) reached out about paid collab + free Bedsure sheet set. Deliverables: 1 IG Reel, 90-day paid ads usage rights, brand tag + hashtag, 2-week link in bio.
deals.activity_note: New deal — Bedsure collab via MakeWonder MCN (Iris at iris@makewondermcn.com). Liz quoted $4,500 bundle on Apr 13, followed up Apr 20 — no brand response yet.
